_metadata.statistical_methods: Independent two-sample t-tests across 5 train seeds. p < 0.05 → statistically significant; p >= 0.05 → trend only. n=5 is small; interpret p-values together with Cohen's d and metric direction. Primary statistical claim: benign_intervention_rate (PPO < DQN, p < 0.05 all modes). Supporting claim: benign_harm_score (PPO < DQN, significant under stress settings). Ratio metrics (mitigation_efficiency, weighted_mitigation_efficiency) describe tradeoff magnitude — not used as primary statistical evidence.
_metadata.dqn_note: sb3_strict_default: buffer_size=1_000_000, learning_starts=100, exploration_fraction=0.1 — all SB3 defaults, no adjustments

metric_definitions.mitigation_rate: % active attack steps with correct mitigating action
metric_definitions.exact_response_rate: % active steps with OPTIMAL_ACTION for that ip_type
metric_definitions.honeypot_capture_rate: % active L7 steps that were Redirected to honeypot
metric_definitions.benign_intervention_rate: % active benign steps with any non-Allow action (RateLimit+Redirect+Block); broad proxy for benign-user disruption, not equivalent to false positive rate
metric_definitions.benign_harm_score: severity-weighted benign disruption: 1×benign_ratelimit + 2×benign_redirect + 3×benign_block; higher = more operational friction on legit users
metric_definitions.over_mitigation_rate: % active steps where action severity > optimal for that ip_type
metric_definitions.action_oscillation_rate: % consecutive appearances of same IP where action changed
metric_definitions.service_damage_auc: mean step_damage per step across all steps
metric_definitions.dynamic_exact_response_rate: % active steps with L7-phase-aware optimal action (Redirect when block_ready=False, Block when block_ready=True)
metric_definitions.l7_redirect_hold_rate: % L7 steps in hold-phase correctly Redirected
metric_definitions.l7_late_under_escalation_rate: % L7 escalation-phase steps still Redirecting after escalation threshold
metric_definitions.safe_intervention_rate: % active steps where action was a correct mitigating response
metric_definitions.mitigation_efficiency: mitigation_rate / benign_intervention_rate — security-usability tradeoff indicator; higher = more attack protection per unit of benign disruption. Computed from aggregate rates for stability. Not a replacement for reward/mitigation as primary metrics.
metric_definitions.weighted_mitigation_efficiency: mitigation_rate / benign_harm_score — same concept with severity-weighted denominator; more conservative than mitigation_efficiency when benign block/redirect occur.
